regulators.name: Monetary Authority of Singapore -- zero-feed: live-verified 2026-07-13 with this project's own honest, descriptive User-Agent (no browser impersonation). Every path tried (the /news listing page, /rss, /rss.xml, /sitemap.xml -- despite robots.txt at mas.gov.sg/robots.txt itself resolving genuinely and pointing to that same sitemap.xml, a category page, and five specific known document/PDF URLs including a direct -/media/ PDF path for MAS Notice PSN02) returned an identical HTTP 200, 853,817-byte Akamai-served HTML shell with <title>Maintenance</title> and body text 'Sorry, this service is currently unavailable... provide the ID number <ref-id>... for further assistance', not the requested content -- a bot-management block applied uniformly regardless of path or content-type requested, confirming PROGRESS.md's P6 finding first-hand rather than by assumption. Registered per the owner's P6 'manual-assisted watcher' decision (site.json coverage_notes): a human or a manual per-item process supplies MAS document URLs/text for the analyst to cite, rather than an automated watcher polling a feed that does not exist.

Singapore Statutes Online (Attorney-General's Chambers' official portal for consolidated Singapore legislation, e.g. the Payment Services Act 2019 and the Financial Services and Markets Act 2022) -- zero-feed: live-verified 2026-07-13 with this project's own honest User-Agent; every path tried (the site root, a specific Act page, /robots.txt, and /sitemap.xml) returned an identical HTTP 403 from CloudFront with body 'ERROR: The request could not be satisfied ... Request blocked', i.e. blocked outright rather than serving a JS-SPA shell -- a stricter block than MAS's fake-200 Maintenance page, and not confirmed fetchable by this project's watcher despite being the authoritative primary source for Singapore statute text. Registered anyway, zero-feed, as an official_domain so the analyst/verifier can cite it when statute text is sourced by a human or by a one-off fetch through some other channel; the pipeline's own automated watcher cannot currently poll it.

Singapore Press Centre (the Singapore Government's official press-release repository; publishes MAS media releases, among other agencies', as genuine downloadable PDFs) -- zero-feed: live-verified 2026-07-13 with this project's own honest User-Agent; repeated direct fetches of www.sgpc.gov.sg/api/file/getfile/... PDF URLs across multiple MAS media releases (2022-2025 dates) all returned genuine HTTP 200 PDF content with extractable text, unlike www.mas.gov.sg's own domain, which serves an identical bot-blocked 'Maintenance' page on every path. This is currently the primary gate-verifiable citation channel for MAS media-release facts in this jurisdiction. Registered zero-feed (no general listing/index page confirmed reliably pollable this session): a human or a one-off fetch supplies the specific release URL for the analyst to cite, per the same manual-assisted-watcher decision covering 'mas'.

Bank for International Settlements -- not a Singapore regulator, but the venue this jurisdiction's content relies on to cite verbatim reproductions of MAS officials' own speeches when mas.gov.sg's own speeches section is bot-blocked (the same 'Maintenance' page as every other mas.gov.sg path). BIS Review republishes central-bank speeches, including MAS's, as an official record of the speech as delivered -- registered zero-feed, narrowly, for this citation-mirror purpose only, matching the sgpc.gov.sg precedent for MAS media releases.

Inland Revenue Authority of Singapore -- has a real but ancillary role in this jurisdiction's digital-asset regime, limited to tax treatment (GST exemption for digital payment token supplies from 1 Jan 2020; income-tax e-Tax Guides on digital tokens), not licensing or conduct regulation, which sits with MAS. Zero-feed: live-verified 2026-07-13 with this project's own honest User-Agent; both the site root (www.iras.gov.sg, HTTP 200, genuine content) and a specific e-Tax Guide PDF (e-tax-guide_gst_digital-payment-tokens.pdf) were fetch-verified this session -- one of the few honestly-fetchable Singapore official domains alongside sgpc.gov.sg and parliament.gov.sg. Registered zero-feed as an official_domain so the analyst/verifier can cite IRAS tax guidance directly; no general watcher built this session.

Parliament of Singapore -- publishes Bill PDFs (e.g. the Payment Services (Amendment) Bill and the Financial Services and Markets (Amendment) Bill) at www.parliament.gov.sg; some individual Bill URLs are genuinely fetchable while others have returned HTTP 403 in prior sessions (inconsistent, not domain-wide), so each Bill URL should be fetch-tested individually rather than assumed. Note: sprs.parl.gov.sg (Singapore Parliament Reports, i.e. Hansard) is a JS-rendered shell and was not confirmed fetchable -- not registered as an official_domain here. Zero-feed: live-verified 2026-07-13 (Payment Services (Amendment) Bill PDF, HTTP 200, genuine PDF content); registered zero-feed as an official_domain so the analyst/verifier can cite specific Bill PDFs when a human or one-off fetch supplies the URL.
